topic.name: Oil and Gas Regulation
topic.description: Legislation concerning the management and taxation of oil and gas extraction from the outer continental shelf.

Document 1: Amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act to declare that any royalty under an oil and gas lease on submerged lands shall apply to all oil removed under the lease.
Increases the minimum royalty under such leases from 12.5% to 20%.

topic.name: Tax Code Amendments
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various amendments to the Internal Revenue Code aimed at modifying tax policies related to education, healthcare, environmental issues, and tax relief measures.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to include qualified elementary and secondary education expenses
Hope Scholarship Tax Credit
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to revise the tax credit for health insurance costs
expand eligibility criteria for such credit
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow a first-year tax deduction of 50% for qualified reuse and recycling property
tax-exempt bond financing for recycling facilities
Document 4: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to deny all income tax credits and deductions to an offending oil polluter
defines 'offending oil polluter'
Document 5: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to establish a permanent 6.0% tax rate on wages paid to employees under FICA
Document 6: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to provide that the standard mileage rate for charitable purposes shall be determined by the Secretary of the Treasury
Document 7: Eliminates the terminating date of the Economic Growth and Tax Relief Reconciliation Act of 2001
makes the tax relief provisions permanent
Document 8: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to increase the unified credit against the estate and gift tax to $5 million
adjust such amount for inflation
Document 9: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to terminate the taxpayer election to designate $3 of income tax liability for presidential election financing
Document 10: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow the issuance of tax-exempt facility bonds through 2012
to make automobile development facilities compatible for qualified alternative motor vehicles

topic.name: Education Policy and Reform
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at improving educational systems, student support, and safety in schools.

Document 1: Amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 to require institutions of higher education to participate in an individual-level integrated postsecondary education data system.
Requires the systems to include earnings data, disaggregated by educational program, institution, degree, employment sector, and state.
Document 2: Amends the School Dropout Prevention program under part H of title I of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965.
Requires state grantees to create and periodically update a research-based Statewide Graduation Success Plan.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Education to award competitive grants to states to develop and carry out statewide pilot programs that randomly test secondary school students for performance-enhancing drug use.
Requires a parent's or guardian's written consent before a student may be tested.
Document 4: Amends title IV of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to require each institution of higher education to include in its annual security report a statement of policy regarding harassment.
Authorizes the Secretary of Education to award competitive grants to IHEs to improve programs to prevent harassment.
Document 5: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to alter requirements for adequate yearly progress assessments of student groups.
Revises criteria for local educational agency identification of schools needing improvement.
Document 6: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to change funding formulas under the Even Start Family Literacy program.
Requires all Even Start instructors to have at least an associate's degree related to early childhood education.
Document 7: Amends title IV of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to modify provisions related to the Federal Pell Grant program.
Increases the maximum Pell Grant award for academic year 2017-2018 and adjusts it in subsequent award years.
Document 8: Requires states and local educational agencies to obtain an FBI background check on individuals prior to offering them employment as school employees.
Prohibits states or LEAs from hiring individuals as school employees if they have been convicted of a crime of violence.
Document 9: Revises subpart 1 of the Charter School Programs of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965.
Requires grantees to use at least 7% of the grant funds to provide technical assistance to subgrantees.
Document 10: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to authorize the Secretary of Education to award matching grants to nonprofit organizations for Pediatric Early Literacy Programs.
Requires health care providers to encourage parents to read aloud to their children.

topic.name: Medicare Policy Amendments
topic.description: This cluster discusses various amendments and acts related to the Medicare program under the Social Security Act, focusing on healthcare services, provider agreements, and beneficiary support.

Document 1: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act
postpone from December 31, 2010, to December 31, 2012
Document 2: Fostering Independence Through Technology Act of 2013
incentives to home health agencies to furnish remote patient monitoring services
Document 3: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act
create an exception to the 60-day limit on Medicare reciprocal billing
Document 4: Medicare Electronic Medication and Safety Protection (E-MEDS) Act of 2007
require the Secretary of Health and Human Services to make incentive payments for physician use of an electronic prescription drug program
Document 5: Affordable Medicines Utilization Act of 2011
Amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act
Document 6: Outpatient Mental Health Modernization Act of 2009
Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act
Document 7: Reducing Emergency Department Utilization through Coordination and Empowerment Demonstration Program Act
establish the REDUCE demonstration program under title XIX (Medicaid)
Document 8: Medicare Audiology Services Enhancement Act of 2013
Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act
Document 9: Securing Care for Seniors Act of 2013
Amends part C (Medicare+Choice) of title XVIII (Medicare)
Document 10: Veterans' Emergency Care Fairness Act of 2009
authority of the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to reimburse a veteran for the cost of emergency care

topic.name: Tariff Schedule Amendments
topic.description: The documents discuss amendments to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States, focusing on the temporary suspension or reduction of duties on various products.

Document 1: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on certain DVD readers and writers.
Document 2: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on a mixture of barium carbonate, strontium carbonate, calcium carbonate, and 1-methoxy-2-propanol acetate, for use as emitter suspension cathode coating.
Document 3: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on Strong Base Anionic Resin-Quaternary amine styrene divinylbenzene copolymer in the chloride form.
Document 4: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on reaction products of phosphorous trichloride with 1,1'-biphenyl and 2,4-bis(1,1-dimethylethyl)phenol.
Document 6: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on certain plastic children's wallets.
Document 7: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to reduce temporarily the duty on boys' knitted or crocheted shirts of man-made fibers.
Document 8: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on aluminum lamp-holder housings containing sockets.
Document 9: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on right angle ground fault circuit interrupters.
Document 10: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on certain synthetic staple fibers that are not carded, combed, or otherwise processed for spinning.

topic.name: Veterans' Benefits and Support
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving the benefits and support services available to military veterans and their families.

Document 1: Amends the Public Health Service Act to revise the grant program to improve emergency medical services in rural areas
provision to military veterans of required coursework and training
Document 2: Requires that a member of a reserve component of the Armed Forces who was deployed for more than 179 days be retained on active duty
provides reintegration counseling and services
Document 4: Authorizes payment under the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) beneficiary travel program of travel expenses
in connection with medical examination, treatment, or care of a veteran
Document 5: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to facilitate collaborative research to meet the long-term reproductive health care needs of veterans
includes fertility counseling and treatment within authorized VA medical services
Document 8: Directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to carry out a two-year pilot program of training, certification, and support for eligible family caregivers
requires caregivers to be paid for providing such services
Document 9: authorizes the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) to place the veteran in a state licensed or certified residential care facility
if the cost of providing such care through the facility is less than the cost of providing the care through any other available option

topic.name: Violence and Crime Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at preventing and addressing various forms of violence and crime, including gun trafficking, sexual assault, child abuse, and domestic violence.

Document 1: Anti-Gun Trafficking Act of 2012
increase criminal penalties for the use of a stolen or altered firearm
Document 2: Sexual Assault Forensic Evidence Registry Act of 2012
establish a Sexual Assault Forensic Evidence Registry
Document 3: Healthy Families Act
care for the medical needs of certain family members relating to domestic violence, sexual assault, or stalking
Document 4: pay the fees charged by any governmental authority associated with obtaining the birth certificate for certain victims of domestic violence
Document 5: amends the Child Abuse Prevention and Treatment Act
investigation of suspected child abuse cases
Document 6: Stand Against Violence and Empower Native Women Act
combat violent crime against Indian women
Document 7: Internet Stopping Adults Facilitating the Exploitation of Today's Youth Act
prohibit financial transactions that facilitate access to child pornography
Document 8: Campus Safety for Children Act of 2012
statistics on sexual offenses against minors
Document 9: No Firearms for Foreign Felons Act of 2013
prevent any individual convicted of a felony from possessing a firearm
Document 10: Child Abuse Reporting Enforcement Act
deny state eligibility for payment if it fails to enact a law making it a felony for individuals to fail to report child abuse cases

topic.name: Housing and Financial Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving housing finance, consumer protection in mortgage lending, and the regulation of financial institutions involved in real estate activities.

Document 1: Amends the National Housing Act to revise the single insurance premium payment for a mortgage of a first-time homebuyer who completes a program of counseling on the responsibilities and financial management involved in homeownership.
Sets the single mortgage insurance premium payment at 25 basis points lower than the premium payment amount otherwise established.
Document 2: Prohibits financial holding companies and national banks from engaging, directly or indirectly, in real estate brokerage or real estate management activities.
Exempts from such prohibition activities of a bank holding company that directly relate to managing any real property owned by national banks or their affiliates.
Document 3: Requires the mortgagee, upon default of a federally related mortgage loan secured by a lien on the borrower's principal residence, to engage in loss mitigation activities that provide for the long-term affordability of the loan.
Prohibits initiation of foreclosure on any mortgage loan if the mortgagee or servicer has at any time failed to comply with specified loss mitigation requirements.
Document 4: Authorizes covered financial institutions to conduct a contest, known as a 'savings promotion raffle,' in which the sole consideration required for a chance of winning designated prizes is obtained by the deposit of a specified amount of money in a savings account or program.
Excludes such a 'savings promotion raffle' from the prohibition against a covered financial institution's dealing in 'lottery' tickets.
Document 5: Amends the Federal Deposit Insurance Act and the Federal Credit Union Act to redefine the standard maximum deposit insurance amount as being $250,000, respectively.
Increases to $100 billion the borrowing authority of the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (FDIC).
Document 6: Directs the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System to publish proposed revisions to the Small Bank Holding Company Policy Statement.
Amends the Truth in Lending Act to require the Board to exempt from certain escrow or impound requirements a loan secured by a first lien on a consumer's principal dwelling.
Document 7: Amends the Consumer Financial Protection Act of 2010 to exempt community financial institutions from all rules and regulations issued by the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau.
Defines a 'community financial institution' as an insured depository institution or credit union with less than $10 billion in consolidated assets.
Document 8: Amends the Sarbanes-Oxley Act of 2002 to exempt from its rules regarding management assessment of internal controls institutions with total consolidated assets of $1 billion or less.
Includes as a qualified mortgage any mortgage loan originated and retained in portfolio for at least three years by a depository institution having less than $10 billion in total assets.
Document 9: Requires the receipts and disbursements of Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac to be counted as new budget authority for purposes of the federal budget submitted by the President.
Subjects to the statutory public debt limit the face amount of obligations issued by Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac.
Document 10: Requires the annual financial disclosure reports of certain federal officers and employees to include mortgages secured by real properties which are the personal residences of the reporting individuals or their spouses.
Requires in the financial report a brief description, the date, and category of value of any purchase, sale, or exchange of such a property during a preceding calendar year.

topic.name: Land Management and Conveyance
topic.description: This topic encompasses the management, conveyance, and designation of public lands by the Bureau of Land Management and the Department of the Interior, including provisions for environmental protection and tribal land trust issues.

Document 1: convey four specified parcels of land in Twin Falls, Idaho, to the city of Twin Falls
support public purposes, including a limited agricultural exemption
Document 2: convey four specified parcels of land in Twin Falls, Idaho, to the city of Twin Falls for use to support public purposes of the Auger Falls Project
Prohibits the use of the conveyed land for residential or commercial purposes
Document 3: provide advance written notification to each owner of land that is adjacent to a parcel to be acquired
to become public lands or National Forest System (NFS) lands
Document 4: Designates certain land under the jurisdiction of the Taos Field Office
as a component of the National Wilderness Preservation System
Document 5: amends the Public Health Service Act to require a state or state-designated entity
to consult with each federally recognized Indian tribe
Document 6: authorize the Secretary of the Interior to take into trust for the Siletz Tribe additional lands
become part of the Tribe's reservation
Document 7: issue in non-wilderness areas within the boundary of the Denali National Park and Preserve
right-of-way permits for the high-pressure transmission pipeline
Document 8: Cape Fox Corporation... shall not be required to select or receive conveyance of specified unconveyed federal land
requires the Secretary of the Interior to convey
Document 9: extends federal recognition to the Lumbee Tribe of North Carolina
authorize the Secretary of the Interior to take land into trust for the Tribe
Document 10: requires the Secretary of the Interior to take lands identified as alternative lands into trust
become part of the Tribe's reservation immediately upon attaining trust status

topic.name: Federal Election Campaign Act Amendments
topic.description: The documents discuss various amendments and provisions related to the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971, focusing on campaign contributions, transparency, and regulations affecting political committees and candidates.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 (FECA) to treat as a campaign contribution any payment made by any person for a coordinated expenditure.

Prohibits candidates or individuals holding federal office, their agents, and certain related entities from soliciting, receiving, directing, or transferring funds to or on behalf of any political committee which accepts donations or contributions that do not comply with FECA limitations.
Document 2: This bill amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 (FECA) to treat as a campaign contribution any payment made by any person for a coordinated expenditure.
Prescribes penalties for knowing and willful violation of this Act by a contribution which consists of a payment for a coordinated expenditure.
Document 3: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to revise the requirement of a 48-hour notification of a campaign contribution of $1,000 or more to the Federal Election Commission (FEC).
Requires Senate candidates to file designations, statements, and reports directly with the FEC.
Document 4: This bill amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to revise the requirement of a 48-hour notification of a campaign contribution of $1,000 or more to the Federal Election Commission (FEC).
Any amount transferred by a joint fundraising committee established by a candidate's authorized committee to any other authorized committee of that candidate shall be treated as a contribution.
Document 5: This bill amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to require any candidate of a major party for the office of President to file with the Federal Election Commission (FEC) a copy of the candidate's income tax returns for the three most recent taxable years.
A tax return furnished to the FEC by a candidate or by Treasury shall be treated in the same manner as a report filed by the candidate.
Document 6: This bill amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to require any candidate of a major party for the office of President to file with the Federal Election Commission (FEC) a copy of the candidate's income tax returns for the three most recent taxable years.
Treasury shall furnish the FEC with copies of any requested returns.
Document 7: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to prohibit any authorized committee of a candidate or any other political committee established from employing the spouse or any immediate family member of such candidate.
Prohibits employing the spouse or immediate family member of a candidate or federal office holder.
Document 8: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to prohibit any authorized committee of a candidate or any other political committee from employing the spouse or any immediate family member of such candidate.
Amends the federal criminal code to subject to specified criminal and civil penalties any spouse of a Member of Congress who knowingly lobbies any Member of Congress.
Document 9: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to revise the requirement of a 48-hour notification of a campaign contribution of $1,000 or more to the Federal Election Commission (FEC).
Applies this requirement to any political committee regarding cumulative contributions of $1,000 or more from any contributor during a calendar year.
Document 10: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to require the principal campaign committee of a candidate for election to the Office of President to include with the committee's statement of organization a copy of the candidate's birth certificate.
Documentation necessary to establish that the candidate meets the qualifications for eligibility to the Office of President under the Constitution.

topic.name: Immigration Policy and Reform
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative measures related to immigration eligibility, verification processes, and protections for immigrants in the United States.

Document 1: Employment Eligibility Verification and Anti-Identity Theft Act - Directs the Commissioner of the Social Security Administration to notify a person or entity each time that the combination of name and Social Security account number it has submitted for an individual does not match Social Security Administration records.
Places the burden of resolving errors in the verification mechanism on the individual whose employment eligibility and identity have not been verified.
Document 2: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to require the Secretary of Homeland Security (DHS) to adjudicate a visa petition filed by an employer on behalf of an alien with extraordinary ability in the arts.
Requires the petitioner to be provided premium-processing services without a fee if such petition is not adjudicated within the required time frame.
Document 3: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act (INA) to provide increased protections and eligibility for family-sponsored immigrants.
Authorizes the Secretary of Homeland Security to deny a family-based immigration petition by a U.S. petitioner for an alien spouse or child if the petitioner is on the national sex offender registry.
Document 4: This bill requires that, in addition to the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) screening, the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) shall take all actions necessary to ensure that each covered alien receives a background investigation before U.S. refugee admission.

A covered alien may not be admitted as a refugee until the FBI certifies to DHS and the Director of National Intelligence that he or she has received a background investigation sufficient to determine whether the alien is a U.S. security threat.
Document 5: Authorizes the Secretary of Homeland Security (DHS) to cancel the removal of, and adjust to the status of an alien lawfully admitted for permanent residence on a conditional basis.
Requires prior to the granting of conditional permanent resident status that an alien satisfies specific criteria including good moral character and educational attainment.
Document 6: Authorizes the Secretary of Homeland Security (DHS) to adjust to permanent resident status an alien who was granted parole admission into the United States pursuant to the humanitarian parole policy for certain Haitian orphans.
Limits the number of such adjusted aliens to 1400.
Document 7: This bill amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to require the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) to enter into a written agreement with a state or a subdivision thereof pursuant to which a state or local officer or employee may carry out immigration-related investigations.
DHS shall ensure that information is provided to the National Criminal Information Center regarding the identity of any individual with an active order of removal.
Document 8: Amends the Illegal Immigration Reform and Immigrant Responsibility Act of 1996 to revise the termination date for the basic pilot employment eligibility confirmation program.
The program is aimed at verifying employment eligibility of individuals.
Document 9: Amends the Haitian Refugee Immigration Fairness Act of 1998 to require determinations with respect to children to be made using the age and status of an individual on October 21, 1998.
Permits new status adjustment applications to be filed for a limited time period.
Document 10: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to establish a nonimmigrant visa category for an alien having a foreign country residence, who is coming temporarily to the United States to work in the seafood processing industry.
This act addresses specific labor needs within the seafood processing sector.

topic.name: Intelligence Community Oversight
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing accountability, transparency, and oversight within the U.S. intelligence community.

1: Amends the National Security Act of 1947 to direct the head of an element of the intelligence community to provide to the Secretary of Defense any intelligence information obtained that indicates harm to the U.S.
Requires the Secretary to report annually to Congress concerning the instances in which such information was provided.
2: Authorizes appropriations for FY2010 for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities of various intelligence agencies.
Requires notification of the congressional intelligence committees in either case.
4: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to prepare an annual assessment for the intelligence community that assesses the use of private contractors.
Prohibits the use of private contractors for the arrest, interrogation, detention, or transportation of persons under government custody.
5: Amends the Intelligence Reform and Terrorism Prevention Act of 2004 to expand the functions of the Privacy and Civil Liberties Oversight Board to include reviews of legislation, regulations, policies, and executive branch actions relating to foreign intelligence.
Allows intelligence community employees to submit whistleblower complaints regarding privacy or civil liberties concerns.

topic.name: Drug and Food Safety Regulations
topic.description: Legislation focused on the regulation, labeling, and safety of drugs and food products to ensure public health.

Document 1: Amends the Controlled Substances Act to define factors relevant to public health and safety for drug registration.
Requires the Attorney General to determine whether denial, revocation, or suspension of drug registration is warranted.
Document 2: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to require nutrition labels on food packages.
Specifies principles for food labeling regulations to ensure clarity and safety.
Document 3: Allows changes to drug labeling for approved generic drugs in line with regulations.
Ensures safety and accuracy in drug information provided to consumers.
Document 4: Revises definitions related to animal drug applications and their review process.
Sets forth user fees and reporting requirements for animal drug manufacturers.
Document 5: Establishes provisions for food safety and drug/device safety regulations.
Requires hazard analysis and preventive controls for food facilities.
Document 6: Authorizes the FTC to address anticompetitive practices in drug manufacturing agreements.
Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act regarding generic drug exclusivity.
Document 7: Requires opioid drugs to prominently state addiction risks on labels.
Focuses on public health concerns related to opioid use.
Document 9: Amends the Controlled Substances Act to regulate synthetic drugs.
Addresses public health risks associated with synthetic opioids and cannabinoids.
Document 10: Prohibits sales of certain cough treatments to minors to prevent abuse.
Imposes penalties for violations related to the sale of dextromethorphan.

topic.name: Mental Health Funding and Services
topic.description: Legislation focused on improving mental health services and funding for various populations, including students and individuals in the justice system.

1: authorizes the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to award grants to eligible entities for the construction or modernization of facilities to provide mental health and substance abuse services
requires a grant recipient to agree to make available nonfederal contributions matching federal funds provided.
2: require the Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration (SAMHSA) to award grants to institutions of higher education to provide and improve mental and behavioral health services
SAMHSA must give special consideration to programs that target underserved and at-risk populations.
3: require the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to award grants to eligible institutions of higher education to improve mental and behavioral health services
directs the Secretary to give special consideration to programs that demonstrate the greatest need.
4: revises and extends through FY2020 grant programs for enhancement of services for students with mental health or substance use disorders at institutions of higher education
training school personnel to recognize symptoms of childhood and adolescent mental disorders.
6: develop a plan to address the service needs of juveniles with mental health or substance abuse disorders who come into contact with the justice system

requires states receiving juvenile justice grants to include in their state plan information regarding screening, assessment, and treatment of juveniles with mental health and substance abuse disorders.
7: authorizes the National Institute of Mental Health (NIMH) to establish a Youth Mental Health Research Network for the conduct or support of youth mental health research and intervention services
conducting youth mental health research or training for researchers in youth mental health research techniques.
9: authorize the Department of Justice (DOJ) to award grants for mental health programs and related law enforcement and corrections programs
DOJ must establish a pilot program to determine the effectiveness of diverting eligible offenders to drug or mental health courts.

topic.name: Rural Agricultural Policy
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving agricultural practices, supporting rural housing, and providing financial assistance to farmers and ranchers in rural areas.

Document 1: direct the Secretary of Agriculture to designate as bioenergy cropsheds areas that are physically located within a 50-mile radius
offer to enter into contracts with producers on a farm in a bioenergy cropshed to provide bioenergy crop transition assistance
Document 2: revise the requirements for consideration as rural areas with respect to assistance for farm housing
serious lack of mortgage credit for lower and moderate-income families
Document 3: appropriate funds for emergency financial assistance to producers on a farm that incurred qualifying natural disaster-caused quantity or quality losses
certain processors in Florida for natural disaster-caused crop and other losses
Document 4: ensure veteran access to HUD housing and homeless assistance programs
expand the supply of supportive housing for very low-income veteran families
Document 5: extend the requirements for consideration as rural areas with respect to assistance for farm housing
Document 6: authorize the Secretary of Agriculture to consider national average market price variations for different classes of wheat
determine wheat producer eligibility for 2007 counter-cyclical payments
Document 8: extend assistance to disadvantaged or beginning farmers or ranchers
provide assistance for agricultural rehabilitation and vocational training for veterans
Document 10: require the Department of Agriculture (USDA) to reduce federal crop insurance payments
disclose details regarding subsidies

topic.name: Iran Nuclear Proliferation Policies
topic.description: Legislative measures and policies aimed at preventing Iran from developing nuclear weapons capabilities through diplomatic, economic, and military means.

Document 1: Support for diplomatic efforts relating to preventing Iran from acquiring nuclear weapons
The United States should continue to support efforts in the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA)
Document 2: It is the policy of Congress that it is in the national security interest of the United States to ensure that Iran halts all uranium enrichment
The United States is capable, willing, and ready to use military force to prevent Iran from obtaining nuclear weapons capability.
Document 5: Imposition of sanctions under the Iran and Syria Nonproliferation Act
Require publication of sanctions applicable to such expanded entities in the Federal Register.
Document 6: Directs the President to appoint a high-level U.S. representative to seek to ease tensions and normalize relations with Iran
Rescind the no contact policy with Iran.

topic.name: Veterans and Retirement Benefits
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to educational assistance, retirement plans, and benefits for veterans and seniors.

Document 1: Accelerated payment of veterans' educational assistance under the Montgomery GI Bill
Provides the same benefit for participants under the survivors' and dependents' educational assistance program.
Document 2: Amends Internal Revenue Code provisions relating to employer-established simple individual retirement accounts (IRAs)

Requires the Office of Financial Education of the Department of the Treasury to develop and implement an outreach plan to educate small businesses on the types and benefits of available retirement plans.
Document 5: Directs the Secretary of Defense to pay a special pension to each person who has received a Gold Star lapel button
as a parent of a member of the Armed Forces who died while serving.
Document 6: Prohibits the receipts and disbursements of the Federal Old-Age and Survivors Insurance Trust Fund from being included in the federal budget baseline
Makes proceeds to the Social Security trust funds from Social Security taxes available solely for OASDI purposes.
Document 10: Require certain employers who do not maintain qualifying retirement plans to make available to their eligible employees a payroll deposit individual retirement account
establish an Automatic IRA Advisory Group to make recommendations regarding investment options.

topic.name: Aviation and Transportation Safety
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing safety and efficiency in aviation and transportation sectors.

Document 1: airport checkpoint wait times should not take priority over the security of the nation's aviation system
an assessment of its staffing allocation model to determine the necessary staffing positions
Document 2: designate a rail right-of-way as a corridor for inter-suburban commuter rail
provide technical assistance to expedite completion of inter-suburban rail service
Document 3: amends the FAA Modernization and Reform Act of 2012
initiate a comprehensive review of existing and any other applicable regulations regarding the air transportation
Document 4: bars the Department of Transportation from disapproving an airport development project grant application
without regard to whether that component operates aircraft at the airport
Document 5: reauthorizes through FY2021 and revises the Airport Improvement Program
eliminates the federal cap on passenger facility charges
Document 6: unfair or deceptive practice for a domestic or foreign air carrier to sell an airline ticket without simultaneously displaying all applicable ticket taxes
impose a fuel surcharge that is not correlated to the price of fuel paid
Document 7: extends through December 31, 2010: increased excise taxes on aviation fuels
makes funds available for airport planning and development
Document 8: allows certain air carriers to use such slots also to provide nonstop air transportation service
between Reagan National Airport and another airport more than 1,250 statute miles away
Document 9: requires public hearings in the affected communities if public hearings on a proposed transaction are held
prohibits the Board from approving mergers if their impacts on safety and on all affected communities outweigh their transportation benefits
Document 10: amends federal aviation law with respect to written assurances required for approval of an airport development project grant application
requires further that the airport owner or operator will compensate the lessee for the costs of relocation, replacement, or removal

topic.name: Fishery Management and Conservation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the management, conservation, and sustainability of fishery resources in U.S. waters.

Document 1: Amends the Magnuson-Stevens Fishery Conservation and Management Reauthorization Act of 2006
Prohibit the requirement that a mechanism be established for specifying annual catch limits
Document 2: Amends the High Seas Driftnet Fishing Moratorium Protection Act
Direct the Secretary of Commerce to urge international fishery management organizations to adopt shark conservation measures
Document 3: Amends the Magnuson-Stevens Fishery Conservation and Management Act to add Rhode Island to the Mid-Atlantic Fishery Management Council
Document 5: Amends the Magnuson-Stevens Fishery Conservation and Management Act to add New York State as a member of the New England Fishery Management Council
Document 9: Directs the Mid-Atlantic Fishery Management Council to submit for approval by the Department of Commerce a modified fishery management plan for the commercial and recreational management of summer flounder

topic.name: Labor Rights Legislation
topic.description: This cluster addresses various legislative acts aimed at protecting labor rights, ensuring fair employment practices, and regulating employer-employee relationships.

Document 1: Amends the Fair Labor Standards Act of 1938 to make the restrictions on oppressive child labor inapplicable to employment of an employee age 16 or 17 by a parent.
employment of an employee age 16 or 17 by a parent, or a person standing in place of a parent, in a logging or mechanized operation owned or operated by that parent or person.
Document 2: This bill amends the Fair Labor Standards Act of 1938 to prohibit discharging or retaliating against any employee because such employee has inquired about, discussed, or disclosed comparative compensation information.
Congress recommits itself to the principles of equal pay for equal work.
Document 3: Amends the National Labor Relations Act to deny the National Labor Relations Board (NLRB) any power to order an employer to restore or reinstate any work, product, production line, or equipment.
Applies the amendment made by this Act to any complaint for which a final adjudication by the NLRB has not been made by the date of enactment.
Document 4: Declares that no predispute arbitration agreement shall be valid or enforceable if it requires arbitration of an employment dispute.
Exempts arbitration provisions in collective bargaining agreements from this Act.
Document 5: Amends the National Labor Relations Act and the Railway Labor Act to repeal those provisions that permit employers to require employees to join a union as a condition of employment.
provisions permitting railroad carriers to require, pursuant to such an agreement, payroll deduction of union dues or fees as a condition of employment.
Document 6: Amends the National Labor Relations Act to revise requirements with respect to the National Labor Relations Board (NLRB).
Increases NLRB membership from five to six.
Document 7: Prohibits the Secretary of the Treasury from providing any discretionary performance award to any employee of the Internal Revenue Service (IRS) with respect to whom there is substantial evidence of misconduct.
Requires the Secretary to consider such a denial or withholding of a discretionary performance award as an action necessary to protect the integrity of the IRS.
Document 8: Amends the National Labor Relations Act to require the National Labor Relations Board (NLRB) to decide, before the election of a labor organization as the exclusive collective bargaining representative of all employees.
Prohibits an election less than 40 calendar days following the filing of an election petition.
Document 9: Repeals specified exceptions to rights of certain Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) employees to engage in collective bargaining.
Requires a final decision of the VA with respect to the review of an adverse personnel action against a VA employee to be issued not later than 60 days after such action has been appealed.
Document 10: Repudiates any assertion that federal employees are not protected from discrimination on the basis of sexual orientation.

Prohibits any federal employee who has authority to take, direct others to take, recommend, or approve any personnel action, from discriminating for or against any federal employee or applicant for federal employment on the basis of sexual orientation.

topic.name: Telecommunications Regulation and Safety
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts related to the regulation, safety, and accessibility of telecommunications services in the United States.

Document 1: Requires the FCC to develop a plan to increase sharing of spectrum between federal and nonfederal government users.
Establishes a program of basic research in advanced information and communications technologies.
Document 2: Requires the FCC to develop and implement a program for continued broadcasting of public safety information in analog television service.
Addresses the transition to digital television and public safety.
Document 3: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to require the FCC to issue regulations to prevent child pornography in video services.
Requires schools and libraries to enforce policies protecting minors online.
Document 4: Directs the FCC to revise regulations for digital audio radio satellite service to ensure national service.
Requires rulemaking to determine if locally oriented services can be provided on nationally distributed channels.
Document 5: Amends the Communications Act to require telecommunication carriers to provide call location information to law enforcement agencies in emergencies.
Establishes a program for training law enforcement on using call location information.
Document 6: Amends the Communications Act to allow closed meetings of FCC Commissioners under certain conditions.
Requires public disclosure of meeting details.
Document 7: Prohibits the FCC from forbearing from requirements for eligible telecommunications carriers under the universal service support program.
Directs the FCC to ensure support for only one telephone connection per household.
Document 8: Requires the Department of Commerce to implement a program for digital-to-analog converter box coupons.
Mandates the FCC to carry out an education campaign on digital television service.
Document 9: Expands the FCC's coordination of interconnectivity among telecommunications networks to include information security technologies.
Directs the FCC to include network security considerations in its oversight procedures.
Document 10: Directs the Department of Commerce to fund local governments for digital TV translators to fill coverage gaps.
Requires collocation of wireless communications equipment on translator towers.

topic.name: Data Privacy and Security Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at protecting personal data, ensuring privacy, and establishing security protocols for sensitive information.

Document 1: Supreme Court review of judgments regarding the legality of surveillance programs and intelligence activities.
Document 2: Health Information Privacy and Security Act requires safeguards for protected health information.
Document 3: Personal Data Protection Act mandates security and notification procedures for sensitive personal information.
Document 4: Federal Agency Data Breach Protection Act establishes policies for data breach notification.
Document 5: Consumer Privacy Protection Act creates offenses for concealment of security breaches involving personal information.
Document 6: Personal Tax Information Protection Act prohibits unauthorized disclosure of taxpayer information.
Document 7: Personal Data Protection and Breach Accountability Act enhances penalties for identity theft and data privacy violations.
Document 8: Safe and Secure Federal Websites Act sets security requirements for federal websites collecting personal information.
Document 9: Legislation requiring safeguards for personally identifiable information in health care exchanges.
Document 10: Health Exchange Security and Transparency Act mandates notification of breaches involving personal information.

topic.name: Wildlife Protection Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various legislative measures aimed at the protection and management of wildlife and their habitats in the United States.

Document 1: prohibit any person from breeding or possessing any live animal of any prohibited wildlife species
extends forfeiture provisions to fish, wildlife, or plants that are bred or possessed.
Document 2: prohibit the shipping, transporting, moving, delivering, receiving, possessing, purchasing, selling, or donation of horses and other equines to be slaughtered for human consumption
increases appropriations to carry out such Act.
Document 5: require the Secretary of the Interior or the Secretary of Commerce, as appropriate, to provide to affected states all data that is used as the basis of a determination on whether a species is an endangered species or a threatened species
requires the appropriate Secretary to use data submitted by a state, tribal, or county government in making such a determination.
Document 8: provide for expedited consultation and conferencing with respect to impacts on endangered or threatened species
relocate from the flood control levee any threatened or endangered species of plant or animal that would be taken in the course of the agency action.
Document 9: require the Secretary of the Interior to use data that are empirical or have been field-tested or peer-reviewed
specifies information required in a petition to add a species to the endangered or threatened list.

contracting officer to consider awarding a contract under the pilot program

consult with the heads of other federal agencies to develop and implement standards for procurement officers

authorize the President to declare a federal energy emergency

contracting under the Small Business Innovation Research Program

topic.name: Government Facility Designations
topic.description: The documents primarily discuss the designation of various government facilities, including post offices and clinics, as well as property conveyance for public use.

Document 1: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 3031 Veterans Road West in Staten Island, New York, as the 'Leonard Montalto Post Office Building.'
Document 2: Authorizes the Secretary of the Army to convey to Derry Township, Pennsylvania, all interest of the United States in a parcel of real property... to permit the township to use such parcel for a regional police headquarters and other public purposes.
Document 6: Designates the community-based outpatient clinic of the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) to be constructed at 3141 Centennial Boulevard, Colorado Springs, Colorado, as the 'PFC Floyd K. Lindstrom Department of Veterans Affairs Clinic.'
Document 4: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 1265 Hurffville Road in Deptford Township, New Jersey, as the 'First Lieutenant Salvatore S. Corma II Post Office Building.'
Document 7: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 100 Commerce Drive in Tyrone, Georgia, as the 'First Lieutenant Robert Wilson Collins Post Office Building.'

topic.name: Election and Voting Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts related to election processes, voting rights, and voter protection in the United States.

Document 1: Requires the Director of the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) to issue guidance to prohibit the download, installation, or use by government employees and contractors of open-network peer-to-peer file sharing software on all federal computers, computer systems, and networks, unless approved in accordance with procedures under this Act.
Requires the Director to report annually.
Document 2: Requires in each election for federal office for inclusion on the ballot after the names of all candidates a separate line for 'None of the Above.'
Requires a new election within 30 days if 'None of the Above' receives the most votes.
Document 3: Requires, with respect to a specified referendum on federal milk marketing orders and related reforms, that if a cooperative association of milk producers elects bloc voting on behalf of its members it shall provide each member with written notice of referendum issues.
the Secretary of Agriculture adjust the association vote to reflect individual producer votes.
Document 4: Amends the Help America Vote Act of 2002 to require each voting system purchased after December 31, 2012, and used in an election for federal office to produce an individual permanent paper record for each ballot that is cast.

Requires the Director of the National Institute of Standards and Technology to establish a program to award cash prizes competitively to eligible persons that advance the research, development, demonstration, and application of voting systems which are specifically designed to enhance accessibility.
Document 5: Amends the Sex Offender Notification and Registration Act to require jurisdictions subject to such Act to prohibit registered sex offenders from accepting a position of employment, including a volunteer position, which by the inherent nature of the position places such sex offender in direct and substantial contact with minors.

Requires the Director of the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) to coordinate with the head of each executive agency to determine which government publications could be made available on government web sites and no longer printed.
Document 6: Prohibits a state that has been redistricted after an apportionment from being redistricted again until after the next apportionment of Representatives.
Requires such redistricting to be conducted through a plan developed by the independent redistricting commission established in the state.
Document 7: Amends the Help America Vote Act of 2002 to prohibit an election official from requiring an individual to provide a photo identification as a condition for receiving or casting a ballot in any election for federal office.
Document 8: Applies to such jurisdictions specified criminal penalties relating to the intimidation of voters, interference by government employees, and voting by aliens.
Document 9: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to require all election-related designations, statements, and reports required to be filed under the Act to be filed directly with the Federal Election Commission (FEC).
Document 10: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to require all election-related designations, statements, and reports required to be filed under the Act to be filed directly with the Federal Election Commission (FEC).

topic.name: Trade Policy and Enforcement
topic.description: This cluster focuses on U.S. trade policy, enforcement of antidumping and countervailing duties, and regulations regarding international trade agreements and practices.

Document 1: Fairness in Respondent Selection Act of 2015
amends the Tariff Act of 1930 to require the administering authority to determine an individual countervailable subsidy rate
Document 2: Enforcing Orders and Reducing Circumvention and Evasion Act of 2010
amends the Tariff Act of 1930 to require the administering authority to initiate an antidumping duty or countervailing duty investigation
Document 8: American Trade Enforcement Effectiveness Act
amends the Tariff Act of 1930 with respect to the administration and enforcement of antidumping (AD) and countervailing duty (CVD) orders
Document 9: Currency Reform for Fair Trade Act
Amends the Tariff Act of 1930 to include as a 'countervailable subsidy' requiring action under a countervailing duty or antidumping duty proceeding

topic.name: Native American and Territorial Governance
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the governance, rights, and recognition of Native American tribes and territories within the United States.

1: Authorizes the Secretary of the Interior to make annual disbursements to the Colorado River Indian Tribes
Amends the Indian Land Consolidation Act to revise legal definitions relating to interests

Reaffirms the special political and legal relationship between the United States and the Native Hawaiian people
5: Establishes the Native Hawaiian Interagency Coordinating Group

10: Requires the Department of Commerce, the Department of the Interior, and federal agencies with recreational travel or tourism functions to update their management plans
Support Indian tribes, tribal organizations, and Native Hawaiian organizations in showcasing their history, culture, and continuing vitality

topic.name: Abortion Legislation and Health Care
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various legislative measures related to abortion, health care provisions, and the rights of health care providers and patients in the context of abortion services.

Document 1: Requires comprehensive health care coverage for children of Vietnam veterans suffering from spina bifida.
Document 3: Prohibits any person from performing an abortion within the District of Columbia except in conformity with this Act's requirements.
Document 4: Prohibits federal funds from being used to cover any part of the costs of any health plan that includes coverage of abortion services.
Document 5: Prohibits discrimination against health care entities that refuse to participate in abortion-related activities.
Document 6: Prohibits federal funding of an entity unless it certifies it will not perform abortions.
Document 7: Prohibits performing an abortion if the probable post-fertilization age of the unborn child is 20 weeks or greater.
Document 8: Expands TRICARE to provide additional pregnancy prevention care for women servicemembers.
Document 10: Requires abortion providers to perform an obstetric ultrasound and provide a medical description of the ultrasound images.

topic.name: U.S. Postal Service Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the operations, financial management, and workforce of the United States Postal Service (USPS).

Document 1: Amends the Chinese Student Protection Act of 1992 to eliminate the provision requiring the reduction of annual People's Republic of China (PRC) immigrant visas.
Document 2: Eliminates scheduled payments for pre-funding the Retiree Health Benefit Fund by the U.S. Postal Service (USPS).
Prohibits USPS from closing or consolidating any post office or other postal facility in an area that has a high rate of poverty or unemployment.
Document 3: Prohibits the U.S. Postal Service from contracting for the delivery of mail on any route with one or more families per mile.
Document 4: Makes the results of an AMP study noncognizable for any purpose unless specified time limit and notice requirements are met.
Document 5: Requires the U.S. Postal Service to bargain with unions representing career Postal Service employees before entering into a contract for mail processing.

Document 7: Ensures reasonable and sustainable workloads and schedules for supervisory and management employees.
Document 8: Amends provisions of federal law relating to Alaska bypass mail transportation.

Document 10: Extends provisions requiring the U.S. Postal Service to issue a special postage stamp for first-class mail that costs not less than 15% more than the regular first-class stamp.

topic.name: Mining and Environmental Legislation

Makes miners, core drillers, and ore transporters who suffer renal cancer or any other chronic renal disease eligible for compensation due to exposure to radiation.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to transfer to the Secretary of the Navy administrative jurisdiction over certain public lands in Imperial and Riverside Counties, California, for inclusion within the Chocolate Mountain Aerial Gunnery Range.
Withdraws the mineral estate of such land from all forms of appropriation under the public land laws.

topic.name: Legislative Amendments and Oversight

Document 1: Amends the District of Columbia Home Rule Act to eliminate congressional review of newly-passed District laws.
Document 2: Amends the Oil Pollution Act of 1990 to require all claims against each responsible party for removal costs or damages under such Act to be presented first to the responsible party or guarantor of the source of the discharge or threat.
Document 3: Amends the federal judicial code to deem a law enforcement officer who is the defendant in a criminal prosecution to have been acting under the color of his office.

topic.name: U.S. Legislative Acts on Industry and Health
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at regulating and promoting innovation, health care, and competition within specific industries in the United States.

Facilitate the creation of domestic jobs in the textile industry.
Document 2: Requires the head of each federal agency to conduct a periodic review of any rule issued by such agency that is determined to be a major rule.

Document 8: Amends the Clayton Act to require, in any civil action alleging that a merger may substantially lessen competition, the burden of proof shall be on the defendant.

Document 10: Amends title XVIII of the Social Security Act to exempt qualified hospital-related durable medical equipment entities from programs for competitive acquisition.

prohibits a federal entity from compelling a covered person to testify or produce any document related to information obtained or created as part of engaging in journalism
Document 9: We the People Act
prohibits the Supreme Court and each federal court from adjudicating any claim or relying on judicial decisions involving state or local laws
Document 10: Better Enforcement for Sexual Assault Free Environments Act of 2013
amends the Uniform Code of Military Justice to repeal the authority of a convening authority to use discretion to either set aside a finding of guilty

topic.name: Federal Regulatory and Funding Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses various federal legislative acts aimed at regulating agency operations, funding allocations, and specific programs across multiple sectors, including housing, energy, and emergency services.

Document 1: Requires federal agencies to review their covered rules and modify, consolidate, or terminate such rules on a periodic basis.
Requires each federal agency to designate a Regulatory Review Officer with responsibility for implementation of this Act.
Document 2: Establishes in the Treasury the National Affordable Housing Trust Fund (Fund).

Directs the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) to establish a formula to make such allocations based on the relative needs of recipients for funds to increase the supply of decent quality affordable housing.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to establish an annual production incentive fee for federal onshore and offshore lands subject to a lease for production of oil or natural gas.
Establishes the Energy Efficiency and Renewable Energy Fund to serve as depository for fees received under this Act.
Document 4: Makes continuing appropriations for FY2012.
Prohibits the use of appropriations, funds, or authority granted under this Act for the Department of Defense (DOD) for new production of items not funded for production in FY2011.
Document 5: Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to award grants to eligible entities for U.S.-Israel cooperative neuroscience research.
Establishes in the National Institutes of Health (NIH) an International Neuroscience-Related Research Advisory Board.
Document 7: Requires all executive branch agencies to include a separate category for administrative expenses when submitting their appropriation requests to the Office of Management and Budget (OMB).
Directs each agency to submit a budget to OMB for administrative expenses that reflects a reduction from its FY2009 requests.
Document 9: Amends the Federal Water Pollution Control Act to prohibit the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) from requiring a permit under national pollutant discharge elimination system permitting requirements for a discharge resulting from the conduct of any silvicultural activity.

topic.name: Health and Economic Policy
topic.description: This cluster encompasses legislative acts related to health services, public health workforce development, and economic measures impacting financial institutions and health savings accounts.

Document 1: Amends the Emergency Economic Stabilization Act of 2008 to grant financial institutions the right to repay TARP assistance.
Eliminates the Secretary's authority to extend TARP beyond its termination date.
Document 2: Amends the Public Health Service Act to revise the definition of 'primary health services' for the National Health Service Corps.
Includes optometrists within the National Health Service Corps programs.
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code regarding the taxation of health savings accounts (HSAs).
Allows Medicare beneficiaries to participate in an HSA.
Document 4: Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services to award grants for nurse-managed health clinics.
Requires clinics to provide comprehensive primary health services for underserved populations.
Document 8: Establishes the Public Health Workforce Scholarship Program to address public health workforce shortages.
Requires the Secretary to award grants to public health agencies for loan repayment programs.

topic.name: Legislation on Environmental and Social Issues
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on U.S. legislative measures addressing environmental protection, social justice, and public resource management.

Document 1: Directs the President to submit to Congress a regional strategy to guide U.S. support for multilateral efforts to eliminate the threat to civilians and regional stability posed by the Lord's Resistance Army (LRA).
Expresses the sense of Congress that the President should support efforts by the people of northern Uganda and the government of Uganda to assist internally displaced people.
Document 2: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to require certain cable system operators to carry PEG-use signals to subscribers without material degradation.
Directs such cable system operators to provide for the transmission of such signals in high definition format or better.
Document 3: Amends the Oil Pollution Act of 1990 to remove the limit on liability of a party responsible for an offshore facility from which oil is discharged.
Makes this Act effective on April 15, 2010.
Document 4: Amends specified law prohibiting the killing of wild birds and wild animals in the District of Columbia to declare that nothing in it shall authorize or be construed to permit the Council to prohibit firearm possession.
Denies the District any authority to enact laws that discourage private ownership or use of firearms.
Document 5: Requires certain vessels operating in U.S. waters to have invasive species management plans.
Establishes the National Invasive Species Council (NISC) to lead and coordinate efforts to minimize the negative effects caused by invasive species.
Document 6: Authorizes the Administrator of the General Services Administration to enter into contracts for the lease of a land port of entry or an international bridge.
Requires the Administrator to transmit to Congress an analysis of the cost effectiveness of leasing.
Document 7: Amends the Energy Policy Act of 2005 to reauthorize and extend funding for a grant program for reducing diesel emissions.
Includes Puerto Rico within the meaning of 'state' under such Act.
Document 8: Amends the Tariff Act of 1930 to waive the requirement that Canadian vessels report immediately to the nearest customs facility upon arrival at any U.S. port.
Document 9: Requires the total budget resources for expenditures from the Harbor Maintenance Trust Fund to equal the level of receipts plus interest credited to such Fund.
Limits the use of such resources to harbor maintenance programs only.
Document 10: Requires the Secretary of the Coast Guard to report on the status of all Coast Guard rulemakings under the Oil Pollution Act of 1990.
Establishes requirements for the promulgation of safety standards for towing vessels.

topic.name: Government Accountability and Oversight
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts aimed at enhancing accountability, oversight, and transparency within governmental and military operations.

Document 1: Amends the National Child Protection Act of 1993 to direct the Attorney General to provide for a program for national criminal history background checks for child-serving organizations.
Amends the PROTECT Act to provide for an extension of the Child Safety Pilot Program.
Document 2: Codifies the establishment of the Office of Complex Administrative Investigations in the Office of the Chief of the National Guard Bureau.
Requires the Chief to submit to Congress an annual report regarding all investigations undertaken by the Office during the preceding year.
Document 5: Expresses the sense of Congress that the effectiveness of Accountability Review Boards would be improved by increasing their independence from the Department of State.
Requires that a Board report and related recommendations be submitted to Congress, in addition to the Secretary.
Document 9: Revises the deadline for comments by the Congressional Budget Office and the Government Accountability Office on information contained in specified quarterly reports.
Amends the Emergency Economic Stabilization Act of 2008 to change from semiannual to annual the required reports to the President and Congress.

topic.name: Government Programs and Initiatives
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at establishing government programs and initiatives for public welfare, environmental protection, and community support.

Document 1: establish a national Silver Alert communications network
coordinate the network with the AMBER Alert communications network
Document 2: establishes the Lower Colorado River Management Conference
develop a Lower Colorado River Pollution Elimination and Ecosystem Restoration Plan
Document 3: establishes the BSA Compliance Fund
make grants to community financial institutions
Document 4: authorize the Secretary of Commerce to make grants to a state or local government
assess the potential for the development or enhancement of business clusters
Document 5: establishes an Office of the Ombudsman in the National Marine Fisheries Service
conducts informal, impartial fact finding and investigations
Document 6: amends the False Claims Act regarding a federal civil action
allows an individual who publicly discloses allegations to recover a portion of any awards
Document 7: establish a strategy to prevent the proliferation of weapons of mass destruction
assessment of gaps in current cooperative nonproliferation efforts
Document 8: provide emergency conservation program assistance
emergency watershed protection program assistance
Document 9: adds new criteria for state approval for educational institutions
the VA may waive requirements for educational assistance
Document 10: establish a program to sustain regional training centers
develop model interdisciplinary undergraduate curricula on recognizing and responding to cases of child maltreatment
